original_hypothesis.title: Denial of Service Window at Exact Presale Start Time
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: There is a logical gap in the presale timing checks. `buyCurvesToken` reverts if `startTime >= block.timestamp`, and `buyCurvesTokenWhitelisted` reverts if `startTime <= block.timestamp`. At the exact block where `startTime == block.timestamp`, neither function can be called.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This creates a 1-block denial of service window where trading is completely halted. While brief, it breaks the invariant that trading should transition smoothly from presale to public sale.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Mutually exclusive boundary conditions (`>=` and `<=`) creating a dead zone

preconditions: Presale is configured with a specific `startTime`
A transaction is mined in the exact block where `block.timestamp == startTime`

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: When `startTime == block.timestamp`, both `startTime >= block.timestamp` and `startTime <= block.timestamp` evaluate to true, causing both functions to revert.
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: One of the checks should be strictly `<` or `>`

analysis_questions: Do both functions revert at `startTime == block.timestamp`?
Can this be exploited for MEV or griefing?
validation_requirements: Attempt to call both buy functions and verify they revert

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Users attempting to snipe the public launch at the exact `startTime` will have their transactions revert. This causes confusion and wasted gas, and delays the actual launch by one block.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Wasted gas for users, temporary trading halt
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Resolves automatically in the next block

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Spike in reverted transactions at launch time

validation_result.impact_summary: Creates a 1-block denial of service window where neither public nor whitelisted users can buy tokens at the exact presale start time.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The `buyCurvesToken` function reverts if `startTime >= block.timestamp`, meaning public sales open strictly after the `startTime`. Conversely, the `buyCurvesTokenWhitelisted` function reverts if `startTime <= block.timestamp`, meaning presales close at or before the `startTime`. When a transaction is mined in a block where `block.timestamp` exactly equals `startTime`, both conditions evaluate to true. As a result, both functions revert, creating a 1-block dead zone where no purchases can be made.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The provided code clearly shows the mutually exclusive boundary conditions. In `Curves.sol` line 214, `buyCurvesToken` checks `startTime >= block.timestamp`. In line 411, `buyCurvesTokenWhitelisted` checks `startTime <= block.timestamp`. This overlap at `startTime == block.timestamp` guarantees that both functions will revert.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: Reverts if `startTime >= block.timestamp`, preventing public buys at the exact start time.
Reverts if `startTime <= block.timestamp`, preventing whitelisted buys at the exact start time.

calling_sequence: User calls buyCurvesToken or buyCurvesTokenWhitelisted at block.timestamp == startTime
Condition `startTime >= block.timestamp` or `startTime <= block.timestamp` evaluates to true
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: When `block.timestamp == presalesMeta[curvesTokenSubject].startTime`, any call to `buyCurvesToken` will hit the `startTime >= block.timestamp` check and revert with `SaleNotOpen()`. Any call to `buyCurvesTokenWhitelisted` will hit the `startTime <= block.timestamp` check and revert with `PresaleUnavailable()`. This completely halts trading for that specific block.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: A user or bot attempts to snipe the token launch by submitting a transaction targeted at the exact block where `block.timestamp == startTime`. The transaction reverts, wasting gas. The actual launch is effectively delayed until the next block where `block.timestamp > startTime`.
validation_result.impact_assessment: The impact is limited to a brief 1-block denial of service and wasted gas for users attempting to buy at the exact launch time. While the financial impact is low, it breaks the invariant of a smooth transition from presale to public sale.
recommended_mitigations: Change the condition in `buyCurvesToken` to `startTime > block.timestamp` to allow public buys at the exact start time, OR
Change the condition in `buyCurvesTokenWhitelisted` to `startTime < block.timestamp` to allow whitelisted buys at the exact start time.
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy Curves contract. 2. Call buyCurvesTokenForPresale to set a startTime in the future. 3. Warp the blockchain timestamp to exactly startTime. 4. Attempt to call buyCurvesToken and observe the revert. 5. Attempt to call buyCurvesTokenWhitelisted and observe the revert.
